pattern: Counting distinct non-empty sequences is a backtracking over letter multiplicities: at each step pick any still-available letter, count it, and recurse on the reduced multiset.
hints: Order of the tiles does not matter, only how many of each letter you have; build sequences from those counts.
At each position choose any letter whose remaining count is positive, count that partial sequence, then recurse with one fewer of it.
Recurse over the frequency map: for every letter with count>0, add one to the answer and descend with its count decremented, restoring it afterward.

edge_inputs.description: one repeated letter limits distinct sequences
all identical tiles give only length-based sequences

stress.description: random short strings over a tiny alphabet to force many repeats
stress.note: alphabet of 3 letters and max length 7 keep the search small and duplicate-heavy
